introduction: This presentation aims to provide an overview of our staff's engagement with MediConn's knowledge management platform in March 2025. We will focus on non-clinical content and company-wide updates browsing data.

subsections.heading: Overview of Knowledge Management Platform
subsections.content:
• The Knowledge Management Platform was launched in 2020 to facilitate internal communication
• It enables seamless sharing of clinical and non-clinical information
• The platform is accessible to all staff members, including virtual care staff
• The platform is compliant with all relevant healthcare regulations
• It supports multiple content formats, including text, video, and audio
• The platform's user-friendly interface has been praised by 90% of our staff in a recent survey
• There were two major updates to the platform in 2024, aimed at improving the user experience
subsections.justification: This slide presents an overview of the platform but does not provide the specific usage data required to answer the DR Question

subsections.heading: Platform Usage Trends: Clinical vs Non-Clinical
subsections.content:
• There has been a 30% increase in usage of the platform for clinical content since 2022
• Non-clinical content usage has remained steady over the past three years
• In 2024, 60% of logins were for accessing clinical content
• The platform's usage peaked in January and July of 2024
• The most accessed non-clinical content is company-wide updates
• The platform's user engagement rate for clinical content is consistently higher than non-clinical content
subsections.justification: This slide discusses general platform usage trends but doesn't provide specific data for March 2025 required to answer the DR Question

subsections.heading: Non-Clinical Content Engagement in March 2025
subsections.content:
• The most viewed non-clinical content in March 2025 was about the company's 5-year strategic plan
• 80% of the staff engaged with non-clinical content related to HR policies
• The least viewed non-clinical content was about the cafeteria menu updates
• The platform's average session duration for non-clinical content was 10 minutes
• The total number of views for non-clinical content was around 2,000 in March 2025
subsections.justification: This slide discusses the engagement with non-clinical content but doesn't provide the number of staff who logged in to view non-clinical content in March 2025

conclusion: Understanding the platform usage trends helps us optimize the content and interface for improved engagement. As a next step, we should consider implementing strategies to encourage more frequent usage among staff.

image_queries.slide_title: Platform Usage Trends: Clinical vs Non-Clinical
